name: Homemade Bread

ingredients.item: a quarter cup of lukewarm water

method: In a bowl of lukewarm water, combine yeast and 1 teaspoon
honey. Set aside for 10 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Spray a large mixing bowl lightly with cooking spray and leave
aside. Knead yeast mixture, buttermilk, remaining 2 tablespoons
honey, melted butter, salt, and half of the flour in a stand mixer
equipped with a dough hook or another large mixing basin.
Continue kneading with the remaining flour until the dough
pulls away from the sides of the bay, about 7 to 10 minutes.
Place in a greased mixing bowl and cover with plastic wrap until
doubled in size.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit and set an oven-safe
pan on the bottom shelf. Fill the pan with 2 cups of boiling
water.
Turn dough out onto a lightly floured board and gently press all
over to eliminate air pockets. Pat into a 9" x 12" rectangle, then
roll into a tight cylinder from the short side. Place dough seam-
side down in a greased 8.5" x 4" loaf pan and gently press to
close seam and tuck in ends—cover and proof for 30 to 45
minutes, or until doubled.
Brush loaf with melted butter and bake for 30 minutes, or until
golden brown and internal temperature reaches 195°.
Allow cooling for 10 minutes before removing from loaf pan
and placing on a cooling rack.
